query: Check our dependency licenses for compliance.
expected_behavior: Collects package licenses (nuget-license / license-checker) into { name, license } JSON
Runs scripts/license-gate.mjs to classify against the SPDX policy
Flags GPL/AGPL as LIC-DENY (critical), permissive licenses produce no finding
Raises [GATE] on critical/high; output is schema-valid
GPL-3.0 → LIC-DENY critical; LGPL-2.1 → LIC-WEAK high; null → LIC-UNKNOWN high; custom → LIC-CUSTOM medium
Findings sorted by severity and schema-valid

query: Is it okay to ship with an LGPL dependency?
expected_behavior: Flags LIC-WEAK and explains the linking-terms condition (dynamic vs static)
Recommends legal review rather than asserting a blanket yes/no
Defers CVEs to dependency-vuln and EOL to lts-check
